message:
feat(core,abi): port crypt salt prefix parser to core::crypt::salt (bd-cs-epic)

Lifts ~34 lines of SHA-crypt salt parsing out of unistd_abi.rs into
pure-safe frankenlibc_core::crypt::salt. The actual SHA-256/SHA-512
key-stretching loops still live in the abi alongside crypt(); this
module covers only the format-specific prefix parsing.

New core surface:
  pub const DEFAULT_SHA_ROUNDS: u32 = 5000;
  pub const MIN_SHA_ROUNDS: u32 = 1000;
  pub const MAX_SHA_ROUNDS: u32 = 999_999_999;
  pub const MAX_SALT_LEN: usize = 16;
  pub fn parse_crypt_salt(salt_bytes, prefix_len) -> (u32, &[u8]);

Behavior 1:1 with the previous abi impl. Rounds value clamped into
[MIN, MAX]; missing/empty/non-numeric rounds decay to DEFAULT_SHA_ROUNDS.
Salt is truncated at MAX_SALT_LEN; the trailing `$` is consumed when
present. Defensive addition: prefix_len > buffer.len() returns
(DEFAULT, empty) instead of panicking on the slice index.

abi changes:
  - parse_crypt_salt becomes a 5-line shim that delegates to core and
    widens the returned u32 rounds to usize for the existing
    crypt_sha256 / crypt_sha512 call sites.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-cs-2):
  - 17 new core::crypt::salt unit tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-cs-epic.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+//! `<crypt.h>` — password-hash building blocks (salt parser today).
+//!
+//! Pure-safe Rust port of the byte-level prefix logic that previously
+//! lived inline in frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs::parse_crypt_salt.
+//! The actual SHA-256/SHA-512/MD5/DES key-stretching loops still live
+//! in the abi alongside the corresponding crypt() entry points; this
+//! module covers the format-specific parsing piece that's reusable
+//! and trivial to test in isolation.
+
+pub mod salt;
